828.reason: [Incorrect lookahead logic: peekSymbol(3) may go out of bounds and the condition doesn't properly check for VarDecl which starts with INTTK/CHARTK followed by IDENFR, not based on LPARENT presence]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: peekSymbol(3) may go out of bounds and the condition doesn't properly check for VarDecl which starts with INTTK/CHARTK followed by IDENFR, not based on LPARENT presence]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: peekSymbol(3) may go out of bounds and doesn't properly distinguish between VarDecl and FuncDef/MainFuncDef since both can start with 'int' or 'char']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(3) assumes at least 3 tokens ahead exist, which may not be true near EOF or for short declarations. Also, VarDecl can start with INTTK/CHARTK followed by IDENFR and then possibly '=' or '[', not necessarily related to LPARENT at position 3. This causes misclassification of VarDecl as FuncDef or vice versa.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(3) without ensuring there are at least 3 tokens ahead can cause IndexOutOfBoundsException or wrong parsing decisions. Also, the condition for identifying VarDecl is flawed since BType starts with INTTK/CHARTK, not determined by absence of LPARENT at position 3]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(3) is unreliable for distinguishing VarDecl from FuncDef since function definitions require 'Ident '(' pattern, but variable declarations may have complex initializers that affect token positions]

836.reason: [Incorrect lookahead logic: peekSymbol(2) may go out of bounds and the condition doesn't properly identify FuncDef which starts with FuncType (VOIDTK/INTTK/CHARTK) followed by IDENFR and LPARENT, not just absence of MAINTK at position 2]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: peekSymbol(2) may go out of bounds and the condition doesn't properly identify FuncDef which starts with FuncType (VOIDTK/INTTK/CHARTK) followed by IDENFR and LPARENT, not just absence of MAINTK at position 2]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: peekSymbol(2) assumes the next token after identifier is LPARENT for FuncDef, but doesn't verify the first token is actually a valid FuncType followed by IDENFR; also fails to handle case where MainFuncDef appears]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(2) assumes at least 2 tokens ahead exist. More importantly, FuncDef starts with FuncType (VOIDTK/INTTK/CHARTK) followed by IDENFR, then LPARENT. The condition should check if the current token sequence matches FuncDef's pattern, not just that the second token isn't MAINTK. This can incorrectly parse MainFuncDef as FuncDef or skip valid FuncDefs.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(2) without proper bounds and using negative condition to detect FuncDef is unreliable. FuncDef starts with FuncType (VOIDTK/INTTK/CHARTK), but this check may incorrectly include or exclude valid cases]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect lookahead logic: checking peekSymbol(2) against MAINTK doesn't properly identify FuncDef since MainFuncDef is a separate production and regular FuncDef starts with FuncType followed by Ident, not necessarily related to MAINTK position]
